topic: humanoid robot supply chain: components, suppliers, actuators, sensors, batteries, semiconductors, end effectors, manufacturing, materials, trade policy

items.title: TrendForce: America owns the robot brains, China the parts
items.summary: The US dominates the intelligence stack through Nvidia, Google DeepMind and OpenAI, while China's localized servo motors, reducers and batteries give it the edge in manufacturing scale, according to TrendForce's new component supply chain influence index — which cites AgiBot going from 5,000 to 10,000 units built in just three months as proof of that scaling speed.

items.title: China ships 20% fewer magnets to the US despite the truce
items.summary: Chinese rare-earth magnet exports to the US averaged 479 tons a month in the first half of 2026, about 20% below the pre-restriction norm, according to US customs data — a squeeze that lands squarely on the permanent-magnet motors inside EVs, factory automation and humanoid actuators. US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer calls Beijing's compliance "not perfect."

items.title: Mimic puts its tendon-driven Hand M1 into production
items.summary: The Swiss startup's five-fingered M1 pairs a 25 kg power grasp with tactile sensing fine enough to feel 50 grams, according to mimic — built in-house in Switzerland as part of a full-stack platform that includes a data-collection wearable. humanoid.guide's hands directory scores it 7/10.

items.title: Renesas organizes its humanoid push around four subsystem domains
items.summary: The chipmaker is structuring its humanoid strategy into brain-and-motion, sensing, actuation and power management — betting suppliers will buy platforms rather than parts in a market it expects, according to Renesas, to double every year through 2030.

items.title: Agility's six-point policy plan puts the component gap first
items.summary: CEO Peggy Johnson urges Washington to close the gap on internationally sourced parts — citing McKinsey data that China holds roughly 90% of permanent-magnet motor capacity — in Agility's recommendations for a U.S. humanoid strategy.

items.title: Robot-hand maker Tesollo starts the IPO process after its Series B
items.summary: The Incheon-based end-effector specialist — whose five-fingered DG-5F-S weighs under 1 kg and costs about 60% less than its predecessor, according to the company — exports to 19 countries and is preparing to list next year.
